description:
HDFC Ergo vs ICICI Lombard vs Aditya Birla Health Insurance | 2026 Honest Comparison & Ratings|
 
If you're buying health insurance for your family in 2026, this is the only video you need. We've done the most complete health insurance comparison of India's top 3 plans, HDFC Ergo Optima Secure, ICICI Lombard Elevate, and Aditya Birla Activ One Max, so you can make a confident decision based on real data, not marketing claims.


This is a detailed health Insurance comparison covering product features, real claim performance, customer service quality, and premium pricing — all backed by independent ILM Ratings with zero commission bias and trusted by brands like phonepe.


You'll get a detailed Optima Secure review explaining the 2x Secure Benefit from Day 1, the Limitless add-on, and its premium structure. We break down ICICI Lombard Elevate covering the Power Booster Sum Insured, Worldwide Cover add-on, and Infinite Care benefit. And we cover Aditya Birla Activ One Max including Health Returns, Zero Premium Renewal, the Sum Insured Multiplier, and Unlimited Care lifetime protection.


We then compare all three plans on room rent rules, pre and post-hospitalisation cover, day care and domiciliary treatments, restore and bonus structures, and waiting periods including the 30-day clause, specified illness waiting period, and the pre-existing disease rule, the fine print that actually decides your claim.


Most importantly, we analyse real claim performance using verified data on settlement speed, claim rejection ratio, incurred claim ratio, complaint ratios, and customer service audits, because the best health insurance plan is only as good as its claims experience. 

Whether you're deciding between HDFC Ergo vs ICICI Lombard, ICICI Lombard vs Aditya Birla, or HDFC Ergo vs Aditya Birla then this single video covers all three matchups with complete data, making it the most thorough health insurance comparison available in India for 2026.


Need help finding the best health insurance plan for your specific needs? Chat with a Beshak expert, a real advisors, not agents, zero sales pressure.
👉 Chat on WhatsApp: http://wa.link/Beshak
📞 WhatsApp: +91 836 954 7450


Disclaimer:
The figures, ratings, and data shared in this video are based on the most recent publicly available information at the time of recording. These metrics are dynamic and may change over time based on the insurer’s performance, regulatory updates, or revised disclosures.
For the most up-to-date and accurate information, please visit Beshak.org or consult a qualified advisor before making any decisions

comments.text:
Just the legacy rating bought Aditya Birla rating to 3rd, inspite of rejection of ICICI being 14%. If we compare legacy to rejection percentage... The ranking seems very unfair. At the end rejection matter much more than legacy 
Thanks for the comparison. Really appreciate your effects 👍🏻
comments.author: @elizabethfernandes7910

comments.text:
Hi. It's a great video
Just a few doubts. How does the legacy rating really matter? In the latter part of the video, you mentioned the you have seen lesser complaints and quicker handling of issues with a company with better legacy. But clearly ICICI Lombard has a very poor CSR. So while choosing aditya birla vs icici how are we suppose to compare the two?
Thanks for all the efforts from your end

comments.text: How is HDFC 4.97 at customer experience vs Aditya birla who was way ahead
comments.author: @FantasyArenaOfficial

comments.text: Had a horrible experience yesterday with a network hospital of Aditya Birla. My Cashless claim of 77k was approved, but after 39k deductions. But it was clearly stated that nothing to be collected from patient. Despite of this hospital didn't let us go out of hospital. Saying either you pay the deducted amount or insurance company will pay. Drama started at 03:00 pm. Hospital TPA insisted us to pressurize . After hundreds of calls, tweets, 112 threat, insurance company sent revised approval of 77k. In the conflict between hospital and insurance company, we were kept hostage. Hospital were kept demanding 39k ransom from insurance company. This was very unprofessional from hospital and insurance company. Fortune Hospital Kanpur very unprofessional behaviour.
comments.author: @ErShriyankOmar

comments.text:
Nice comparison video, but I don't understand one thing, sir.

In the customer service category, Aditya Birla has a quick response on their toll-free line, which is the best among the three, and the percentage of complaints is 0. So how did HDFC get better ratings?

In the final rating, HDFC got a higher rating, probably due to marginally better CSR, but how on earth did ICICI Lombard get better ratings than Aditya Birla, considering ICICI Lombard has the worst CSR and product benefits among the three, and a worse percentage of complaints?

Kindly reevaluate the ratings.
comments.author: @madhavvarshney3912
